PicoClaw 项目动态日报 | 2026-07-11

今日速览

今日PicoClaw项目社区活跃度显著提升，特别是在Pull Request（PR）方面，单日新增18个PR，其中大部分为高质量的新提交。Issues方面相对平静，但修复了一个关于WhatsApp Websocket超时的问题。值得注意的是，多位贡献者同时提交了关于代码优化（重构）、安全加固以及WhatsApp功能增强的PR，显示项目正朝着更健壮、更高效的方向发展。当前积压的17个待合并PR也表明，维护者面临的合并压力较大，但这同时也暗示了社区贡献的热情高涨。

版本发布

  • 无新版本发布。 最近一次发布活动暂无记录，项目可能正处于一个大的功能合并周期前的准备阶段。

项目进展

今日有 1 个重要 PR 被关闭/合并，同时有 17 个新 PR 被创建，这表明项目在多个方面同时取得了进展。

  • 已合并/关闭

    • [CLOSED] #3179 [stale] fix(whatsapp): reconnect after websocket drops：修复了WhatsApp WebSocket断开后无法重连的问题。这项合并是三周前提交的（Stale），今日终于被合并，解决了影响WhatsApp通道稳定性的核心问题。(#3179)

  • 新提交且推进较快

    • 安全与鲁棒性
      • #3248 fix: bump Go to 1.25.12：通过升级Go版本来修复标准库中的两个安全漏洞（crypto/tls, os），直接提升了软件安全性。(#3248)
      • #3246 fix: security and robustness hardening：修复了MQTT TLS证书验证被忽略（默认跳过）、OAuth超时未设置等三个安全问题，极具工程价值。(#3246)
    • 核心功能增强
      • #3242 feat(whatsapp): add native typing presence：为WhatsApp通道增加了“正在输入”状态指示，大幅提升了用户交互体验。这是对Issue #3240的响应。(#3242)
      • #3241 fix(auth): make OAuth refresh provider-correct：解决了OAuth刷新令牌时因不同供应商API差异（如OpenAI要求JSON，Google要求表单）导致的失败问题，并修复了并发竞争条件。(#3241)
    • 性能优化与代码重构
      • #3245 / #3244 / #3243：贡献者corporatepiyush提交了一系列重构PR，针对XML转义、字符串拼接等路径进行内存和性能优化，显著减少了内存分配，体现了对代码质量的追求。(#3245, #3244, #3243)
      • #3222 refactor(deltachat)：对DeltaChat通道进行了大规模重构（-320行代码），清理了过时的功能和依赖，提升了可维护性。(#3222)
    • 硬件支持
      • #3205 fix: support 9router gateway：为树莓派等ARMv7设备提供了构建支持，并适配了特有的AI网关响应格式，拓展了硬件兼容性。(#3205)

社区热点

今日讨论最为集中的话题是 WhatsApp通道的体验提升 和 OAuth认证的兼容性问题。

  • WhatsApp体验增强 (#3240, #3242)

    • 链接： Issue #3240 | PR #3242
    • 核心诉求： 用户反馈使用WhatsApp时，发送消息后界面毫无反应（没有“正在输入”提示），这在处理耗时较长的AI回复时体验极差。这直接促使了功能请求Issue #3240的提出。贡献者greencabe在同一天即提交了PR #3242，展现了社区的高效响应能力。用户期望的是一个更接近原生聊天应用的即时反馈体验。

  • OAuth供应商兼容性问题 (#3239, #3241)

    • 链接： Issue #3239 | PR #3241
    • 核心诉求： 用户（贡献者greencabe）在集成不同OAuth供应商时，发现了auth.RefreshAccessToken函数的通用实现存在问题，特别是OpenAI和Google的刷新请求格式不一致。该Issue指出了具体的运行时错误和并发竞争的潜在风险。这个问题的发现非常专业，表明社区用户正在深度使用并与复杂的第三方API集成。PR #3241已针对此问题提出修复方案。

Bug 与稳定性

今日报告的Bug数量少且影响范围集中，但专业性较强。

严重程度 Issue/PR 标题 状态 分析
中 #3239 OAuth refresh requests use incompatible provider semantics and can race 已修复 (PR #3241) 功能Bug。会导致特定OAuth供应商（如OpenAI）的令牌刷新失败，影响用户登陆和API授权。修复方法很明确，将供应商处理和并发安全都考虑了。
低 #3178 [CLOSED] [stale] WhatsApp Websocket Timeout 已关闭 (无PR) 这是一个历史Bug，由于长时间无活动被自动标记为Stale并关闭。相似问题可能已被PR #3179解决，但可能需要关注是否有遗留情况。
中 #3246 fix: security and robustness hardening (MQTT TLS, OAuth timeouts) 修复PR已提交 多个安全/健壮性问题。包括默认关闭TLS证书验证（高危）、未设置超时等。这些问题若不修复，会成为潜在的攻击入口或导致服务不稳定。

功能请求与路线图信号

今日的功能请求主要围绕 体验增强 和 硬件支持。

  1. WhatsApp “正在输入” 状态 (#3240)

    • 描述： 为WhatsApp原生回复增加打字状态显示。
    • 路线图信号： 强烈。这是一个用户最直接的体验痛点和需求。PR #3242已同日提交，预计有很大概率会合入下一个版本。

  2. Linux ARMv7 构建目标 (#3205)

    • 描述： 增加对树莓派等ARM Linux设备的构建支持。
    • 路线图信号： 较强。这表明社区用户有在IoT和边缘设备上部署PicoClaw的强烈需求。该PR已提交并包含了对9router网关的兼容性修复，进展良好。

  3. 可配置的默认模型回退链 (#3200)

    • 描述： 允许用户在Web UI中设置模型回退顺序。
    • 路线图信号： 中等。这是一个提升用户体验和管理灵活性的功能，虽然已提交一周多，但尚未被合并，可能需要维护者评估其整体设计。

用户反馈摘要

  • 正面反馈（隐含）： 大量围绕代码优化和安全性提升的PR提交（如#3245， #3246），表明核心用户和贡献者对代码质量和安全性有较高追求，这是项目健康度的积极信号。
  • 痛点反馈：
    • WhatsApp交互体验不佳： 用户在没有“正在输入”提示的情况下等待AI回复，体验不佳。（Issue #3240）。这是对即时反馈的迫切需求。
    • OAuth集成困难： 开发者用户指出与不同OAuth供应商（特别是OpenAI）的集成存在兼容性问题，导致认证失败。（Issue #3239）。这影响了项目与主流AI服务的连接能力。
    • 硬件支持不足： 用户希望在树莓派等设备上运行PicoClaw，但发现缺乏ARM构建和特定网关的兼容性。（PR #3205）。这限制了项目的部署场景。

  • 长期未合并的Agent协作功能：

    • PR #2937： Feat/agent collaboration， 创建于2026-05-24， 状态 [stale, OPEN]。这是一个提供Agent间协作总线的基础设施级PR，涉及邮件、会话、消息队列等复杂概念。该PR已接近两个月未被合并，可能存在较大的设计分歧或需要更严格的审查。维护者需关注其进展，以决定是合并、打回还是需要重构。(#2937)

  • 多个由bot创建的依赖更新PR处于Stale状态：

  • 新功能合并积压严重：

NanoClaw — qwibitai/nanoclaw

NanoClaw 项目动态日报 | 2026-07-11

1. 今日速览

过去24小时 NanoClaw 项目保持高活跃度：共处理 3 个 Issue（1 个新开/活跃、2 个关闭）和 26 个 PR（待合并 15 个、已合并/关闭 11 个）。项目核心团队在 时间戳统一规范、渠道默认行为重构 及 持久化内存系统 三条主线并行推进，其中 6 个核心 PR 同日合并，表明项目正处于密集集成冲刺阶段。社区方面，共享技能重构遗留问题（#3001）和 CLI 创建流程缺陷（#2415）获得持续关注，稳定性和兼容性仍是当前重点。

2. 版本发布

无新版本发布。上一个正式版尚未标记，建议关注官方 release 页获取通知。

3. 项目进展（今日合并/关闭的重要 PR）

以下为过去 24 小时 已合并或已关闭 的核心 PR，按功能域归类：

🎯 时间戳统一规范（核心修复）

  • #3006 [CLOSED] — 全仓库时间戳规范：存储统一为 ISO-Z UTC，显示转换为本地时区。覆盖 messages_out、logbook、files 等所有 agent 可见时间戳表，消除跨时区显示不一致。
  • #3005 [CLOSED] — 修复 insertTaskRow 时间戳偏移：将 datetime('now')（朴素 UTC）改为 ISO 字符串，使任务时间与聊天时间戳对齐。
  • #3007 [CLOSED] — 修复 Codex/OpenCode 归档文件（conversations/*.md）时间戳：Archived: 行和文件名日期改为安装时区显示，消除晚间会话的日期前缀混乱。

🧩 渠道默认行为声明式重构

  • #3010 [CLOSED] — 核心架构重构：将渠道行为的硬编码/启发式判断（engage 模式、回复线程、自动创建组策略）声明式地 交由各渠道适配器自身配置，降低核心代码耦合。
  • #3011 [CLOSED] — 渠道分支配套实现：为所有适配器添加 ChannelDefaults 声明，修复 WhatsApp 共享号码解析问题。
  • #3009 [CLOSED] — 将渠道格式化技能（WhatsApp、Slack）从主分支移至渠道分支，避免未连接渠道的 Agent 加载无效技能文件。

🛠 开发者工具与文档

  • #3004 [CLOSED] — 新增 scripts/context-preview.ts 工具：模拟首次消息、后续对话、任务触发等 8 种场景，精确输出 Agent 实际看到的完整上下文（CLAUDE.md、系统提示、MCP 工具、挂载点等），极大提升调试效率。
  • #3003 [CLOSED] — 文档改进：要求 agent-browser 技能中自定义条件必须使用有界等待（受 --max-wait 限制），防止 Agent 编写无界轮询导致无限循环。

🧪 其他重要关闭

  • #3015 [CLOSED] — 修复实时进度条：解决 Claude 工具事件早于阶段说明导致的孤立“已完成读取”卡，以及 warning 摘要吞没测试通过数问题，新增 1267 项回归测试通过。
  • #2415 [CLOSED] — Bug 修复：修复 ncl groups create 跳过 container_configs 行导致首次生成失败的回归问题。

总体评价：今日共合并/关闭 11 个 PR，主要贡献来自核心团队 gavrielc（5 个合并）。项目在代码质量（时间戳规范、测试覆盖）、架构灵活性（声明式渠道配置）和开发者体验（context-preview 工具）上有系统性的提升。

条目 类型 互动亮点 链接

好的，作为一名AI智能体与个人AI助手领域的开源项目分析师，这是根据您提供的NullClaw项目数据生成的2026年7月11日项目动态日报。

NullClaw 项目日报 | 2026-07-11

1. 今日速览

今日项目活跃度处于低水平。过去24小时内无新的Pull Request提交或合并，也无新版本发布，核心开发节奏放缓。主要社区活动集中在两个新提交的Issue上，分别暴露了Telegram渠道的稳定性问题和A2A协议的安全漏洞。虽然后端管道运行正常，但前端Agent的可靠性和核心API的安全防护能力成为当前社区关注的焦点。

2. 版本发布

无

3. 项目进展

过去24小时内无任何Pull Request被合并或关闭。项目在代码层面无新功能推进或Bug修复。核心开发工作可能进入静默期或聚焦于尚未公开的分支上。

4. 社区热点

今日讨论主要集中在新开的两个Issue上，虽无大量评论，但所提及的问题性质较为严重。

  • Issue #974: [BUG] NullClaw shared bearer A2A route allows cross-caller task and context reuse

    • 链接: Issue #974
    • 分析: 该 Issue 揭示了A2A（Agent-to-Agent）路由中的一个严重安全缺陷。尽管使用了Bearer Token进行认证，但后续的任务和会话权限校验不充分，导致持有相同Token的不同用户可以访问彼此的任务历史和上下文。这直接违反了多租户安全隔离原则，如果属实，将是一个高危安全漏洞。

  • Issue #972: [BUG] telegram channel stop respond after some idle time

    • 链接: Issue #972
    • 分析: 此问题报告了Telegram Agent在经过一段时间空闲后停止响应，但后端Engine仍在正常工作的情况。这表明问题可能出在Telegram适配器（Channel）层的会话保持或连接池管理上，而非核心推理逻辑。该问题影响用户交互的可用性，是实际使用场景中的关键体验痛点。

5. Bug 与稳定性

今日无新增Bug被修复，但报告了两个新问题：

  • 严重程度：高

    • Issue #974: [BUG] A2A路由安全漏洞，允许跨调用者数据访问。
    • 状态: 未修复，无关联PR。
    • 影响: 若系统用作多用户服务，可能导致严重的数据泄露。

  • 严重程度：中

    • Issue #972: [Bug] Telegram渠道长时间空闲后失效。
    • 状态: 未修复，无关联PR。
    • 影响: 影响Telegram用户的连续使用体验，需要手动干预或重启相关服务。

6. 功能请求与路线图信号

本次数据中无明确的新功能请求。然而，Issue #974 提到的A2A权限问题间接指向了未来路线图中可能的重要方向：建立更细粒度的访问控制模型。从技术角度看，仅依赖Bearer Token是不够的，后续需要考虑引入基于用户/会话的隔离机制或细粒度的权限Token（如OAuth2或JWT的子声明）。

7. 用户反馈摘要

从有限的Issue评论中可以提炼出以下用户反馈：

  • 用户痛点:

    • 稳定性问题: 用户明确表达了对Telegram渠道“die away”的困扰，表明这个长期运行的服务存在连接不稳定的问题。
    • 安全性顾虑: 用户通过Issue #974的PoC（概念验证）明确指出了当前A2A路由在权限校验上的设计缺陷，表达了对数据安全和隔离的担忧。

  • 满意点:

    • 用户在Issue #972中提到，nullclaw的后端引擎在Telegram失效时仍然“work well at backend”，说明核心内存与检索演进（hybrid retrieval）的稳定性得到了认可。

8. 待处理积压

考虑到今天的活跃度较低，以下问题值得维护团队关注：

  • 长期未响应的重要Bug: 虽然没有长期积压，但新出现的Issue #974 (A2A安全漏洞) 属于高优先级问题，应被立刻纳入待办队列，需要维护者尽快确认、评估影响并提供修复时间线，否则可能影响用户对NullClaw安全性的信任。
  • 可用性Bug: Issue #972 (Telegram失效) 虽然不是安全性问题，但直接影响了主要交互渠道的可用性，对于依赖此渠道的用户体验打击较大，建议尽快安排修复。
IronClaw — nearai/ironclaw

好的，作为 AI 智能体与个人 AI 助手领域开源项目分析师，以下是基于 IronClaw 项目 2026-07-10 至 2026-07-11 期间 GitHub 数据生成的日报。

IronClaw 项目日报 (2026-07-11)

1. 今日速览

今日项目活跃度极高，社区反馈密集，尤其是 Issue 报告数量大增，表明 QA 测试或用户前期使用正在大规模展开。核心团队积极响应，一天内关闭了 8 个 Issue 并合并/关闭了 15 个 PR，其中包含一个紧急的启动崩溃修复。整体上，项目正处于一个 高强度的 Bug 扫荡与核心稳定性加固阶段，开发节奏快，但短期内的用户侧稳定性（尤其是 Slack、Routine 和 UI 交互方面）承受了较大压力。

2. 版本发布

今日无新版本发布。

3. 项目进展

今日核心团队合并了多项关键 PR，重点聚焦于 运行时稳定性和执行可靠性：

  • 修复启动崩溃：#5967 修复了由于陈旧的扩展清单导致 Reborn 在启动时崩溃的问题（#5966），该问题会阻塞服务部署。
  • 增强循环执行器韧性：#5959 和 #5960 被提出，旨在解决因模型提供商 5xx 错误和工具调用过多导致的运行中断。#5960 将默认迭代上限从 32 提升至 256，以支持更复杂的工具密集型任务。
  • 修复上下文压缩（Compaction）失败：#5895 是今日最重要的稳定性修复之一。它将非取消类的压缩错误视为可恢复的提示步骤跳过，而非导致运行彻底失败的终端错误，解决了工具调用成功后仍会失败的核心问题。
  • 优化模型行为：#5844 和 #5817 被合并。前者指导模型在需要计算时优先使用工具而非“脑补”；后者修复了模型网关会错误地将十进制数字视作不可用能力 ID 的问题，从而避免误杀正常的工具调用。
  • 重构与清理：移除了对旧版 v1 测试和代码的引用（#5828， #5835），标志着项目正在稳步推进从 v1 向 Reborn 的迁移。

4. 社区热点

今日讨论最集中的 Issue 大多源于 bug_bash（Bug 扫荡） 活动，反映了用户在集成和 UI 交互方面的痛点：

  • #5885：审批通知无法显示审批内容（评论: 2）

    • 链接: https://github.com/nearai/ironclaw/issues/5885
    • 诉求分析：用户点击“需要审批”的通知时，只能看到运行中的动作页面，而审批卡片缺失，导致无法进行任何批准或拒绝操作。这是一个严重阻碍工作流程的 UI Bug，表明核心交互路径存在缺陷。

  • #5948：助手错误地报告 GitHub 扩展已激活（评论: 5）

    • 链接: https://github.com/nearai/ironclaw/issues/5948
    • 诉求分析：这是今日评论最多的 Issue。助手宣称扩展“已安装并配置/激活”，但实际上扩展仅处于“已安装”状态，尚需手动激活。这暴露了助手的状态感知与实际系统状态之间存在严重不一致，会严重误导用户。用户期望助手能提供准确、可操作的反馈。

  • #5879：错误提示横幅在后续成功运行后依然残留（评论: 2）

    • 链接: https://github.com/nearai/ironclaw/issues/5879
    • 诉求分析：UI 未能在新事件发生后正确清除过期状态。这是一个用户体验问题，可能导致用户对系统状态产生持续性的困惑和不信任。

5. Bug 与稳定性

今日报告的 Bug 密集且严重，主要集中在以下几个高优 P1/P2 类别：

严重程度 Issue # 描述 修复状态
P1 #5943 Slack DM 错误地发布到当前频道而非用户私信。 无关联 PR，待修复
P1 #5946 助手在发现所需触发器不可用前，先修改了 Google Sheet，产生脏数据。 无关联 PR，待修复
P2 #5944 Slack DM 静默投递失败，但运行报告成功。 无关联 PR，待修复

此外，#5966（启动崩溃）和 #5838（上下文压缩失败）这两类严重影响可用性的 Bug 已在今日通过 #5967 和 #5895 得到修复。

6. 功能请求与路线图信号

今日有大量新功能的 PR 被提出，预示着项目的下一阶段路线图正在快速成形：

  • 持久化记忆系统：#5974 提出了“情景记忆”功能，旨在为 Agent 提供跨会话的连续对话能力和摘要回忆。这是一个重要的 AI 体验提升，很可能成为核心特性。
  • MCP 超时与后台任务桥接：#5973 为 MCP 服务器引入了可配置超时和后台作业桥接能力，旨在解决长时间 MCP 工具调用导致的中断问题。这是对 #5741 等 Issue 的深层技术回应。
  • 按轮次检索工具：#5972 提出了一个动态工具选择机制，模型每轮只接收核心工具集和基于语义检索的 Top-K 工具，有望大幅减少长提示词、提升模型效率和响应速度。
  • 精细化错误分类：#5954 提出了 RunFailureReason 分类框架，为未来实现自动重试、更好的用户体验反馈奠定了基础。

7. 用户反馈摘要

从今日的 Issue 和 PR 讨论中，可以提炼出以下用户和核心贡献者的反馈：

  • 痛点- Slack 集成混乱：用户对 Slack 集成的体验评价较低。问题集中在：难以断开连接（#5747， #5834），DM 发送失败或错发（#5943， #5944）。这表明 Slack 通道的可靠性和用户意图理解方面存在重大缺陷。
  • 痛点- 助手行为不一致：用户多次反馈助手的声明与实际行为不符，包括错误地报告扩展状态（#5948）、过早执行操作（#5946）以及拒绝执行明确请求（#5834）。这表明助手的上下文理解、规划能力和反馈机制都需要大幅改进。
  • 核心贡献者关注点- 韧性：从 #5959， #5960， #5965 等 PR 可以看到，核心团队正集中精力解决运行时在各种失败场景下的韧性问题，如网络抖动、工具超时、模型提供商错误等。这是提升项目成熟度的关键。
  • 开发者体验- MCP 集成需求强烈：#5968 报告通用 HTTP 工具无法与缺乏 MCP 的第三方服务集成，而 #5973 等 PR 则展示了社区对完善 MCP 基础设施的强烈兴趣。

8. 待处理积压

2. 版本发布

版本号： LobsterAI 2026.7.10 发布时间： 2026-07-10 核心更新：

  • 新功能 (feat):
    • 代理子代理协作 (feat(agents): support delegated subagent collaboration)：支持创建和管理子代理的协作模式，提升了复杂任务的处理能力。
    • 最小化权限提示 (feat(cowork): add minimizable permission prompts)：协作任务的权限请求窗口现可最小化，改善了用户体验。
    • 文件夹上下文附件 (feat(cowork): add folder context attachments)：用户现在可以将本地文件夹作为附件添加到Cowork会话中，系统会以文件夹路径形式处理而非上传所有文件内容。
  • Bug修复 (fix):
    • 窗口标题栏Logo压缩：修复了Windows下侧边栏折叠并带有更新徽标时，标题栏Logo被压缩的问题。
    • 排队跟进协调器连接：修复了跨会话以及在应用最小化时，排队跟进任务无法正确处理的问题。
    • 会话列表中选定队列的提交：修复了仅提交选中的排队的Steer任务，同时保留FIFO处理逻辑。
    • AskUser最小化状态丢失：修复了AskUser交互窗口在最小化后状态丢失的问题。
    • 记忆索引迁移：修复了仅迁移全文搜索（FTS）索引的问题，现在会为所有配置的Agent进行完整的记忆索引处理。
    • IM群任务路由：修复了定时任务中，企业微信和钉钉群ID大小写被错误转换、以及钉钉连接器吞掉错误结果导致误报的问题。
  • 其他：
    • 构建兼容性：修复了构建过程中，空字节剥离与ES2020标准不兼容的问题，确保在旧版Node环境下的稳定性。

破坏性变更 & 迁移注意事项：

  • 本次发布主要包含增强和修复，未报告明显的API或数据格式破坏性变更。
  • 注意： 建议用户在更新后，重启应用以触发对新版本记忆索引功能的自动迁移。如果重启后仍遇到任何与Agent记忆相关的问题，可尝试手动运行 memory index --force 命令。

3. 项目进展

今日项目核心进展围绕昨日发布的 2026.7.10版本 展开，共完成了10个PR的合并/关闭，主要推进了以下方面：

  • Cowork协作能力增强:

    • feat(cowork): add folder context attachments (#2310)：增加了文件夹作为上下文附件的能力，显著提升了文件密集型的协作场景。
    • fix(cowork): connect queued follow-up coordinator (#2315) & fix(cowork): submit only the selected queued steer (#2313)：修复了排队任务的多个问题，优化了异步任务处理流程。
    • fix: askuser minimize state loss (#2312)：修复了AskUser窗口状态问题，提升了交互稳定性。

  • IM集成与定时任务修复:

    • fix(scheduled-task): repair IM group task routing (#2306) & fix(scheduled-task): preserve WeCom and DingTalk group ID casing (#2314)：系统性地修复了企业微信和钉钉集成中的群组ID大小写问题，以及任务路由问题，这对于依赖IM群聊自动化的用户至关重要。

  • 记忆系统优化:

    • fix(memory): migrate fts-only indexes for all agents (#2311)：主动扫描并修复了只拥有FTS索引的Agent，确保所有Agent的记忆系统都能正常工作，提升了长期对话的上下文一致性。

  • 构建与稳定性:

    • fix(build): keep null-byte stripping ES2020-compatible (#2309)：修复了构建系统的兼容性问题。

4. 社区热点

今日最受关注的Issue是 #2293 [OPEN] 重启后，多个agent下的USER.md被覆盖替换的BUG？。

  • 热度分析：该Issue虽然创建于7月7日，但在7月10日有更新，并获得了3条评论。用户抱怨在多Agent环境下，修改一个Agent的“关于你”内容会导致所有Agent的USER.md被同步覆盖。这直接影响到用户为不同Agent设置独立人格/上下文的核心功能。
  • 背后诉求：用户的核心诉求是 “Agent配置的独立性与隔离性”。这反映了用户正在从单Agent测试向多Agent协作的深度使用场景过渡。他们期望每个Agent拥有完全独立的身份和知识库，而当前的设计缺陷严重阻碍了这一使用模式。此问题若不能及时解决，将直接打击高级用户的信任和粘性。

5. Bug 与稳定性

严重程度 Issue / PR 描述 状态
高 #2293 重启后，多Agent下USER.md被主Agent内容覆盖，导致Agent配置无法独立。 待处理 Fix PR
中 (已通过 #2316 修复) Windows标题栏Logo压缩问题。 已修复 (v2026.7.10)
中 (已通过 #2315 修复) Cowork中排队跟进任务连接失败。 已修复 (v2026.7.10)
中 (已通过 #2311 修复) 记忆索引仅对部分Agent生效。 已修复 (v2026.7.10)
低 (已通过 #2306 修复) IM群组（企微/钉钉）定时任务ID大小写问题。 已修复 (v2026.7.10)

6. 功能请求与路线图信号

  • 会话列表按时间分组：Issue #1337 和对应的修复PR #1338 长期开放。该功能请求参考ChatGPT等产品，建议将会话列表按“今天/昨天/本周”分组。鉴于其长期处于待合并状态，且是一个常见的用户体验改进点，很可能在未来版本中被优先合并。
  • MCP自定义服务器JSON导入：PR #1336 提出为MCP服务器配置新增JSON粘贴导入功能，可显著降低高级用户的配置门槛。此功能符合解放生产力的社区呼声，是路线图中值得关注的候选。
  • 定时任务“工作日”选项：PR #1335 新增了“工作日”（周一至周五）的定时任务选项，这是一个非常实际的需求，能更好地满足企业用户的自动化场景。

建议： 上述三个功能（#1338, #1336, #1335）均已实现且处于待合并状态，建议项目维护者尽快评审并合并，以满足用户对基础UI和功能易用性的期待。

7. 用户反馈摘要

从Issue #2293的评论中，可以提炼出以下用户痛点：

用户痛点：“我建立了多个agent，只要改了一个agent的‘关于你’内容，其他agent也同步修改了……重启后，所有agent的USER.md都会被main agent的内容替换。”

用户反馈的核心痛苦点在于：

  • 配置隔离性不足：用户为不同场景或角色配置不同Agent的设想落空，所有Agent的个性化设置变得无效。
  • 数据丢失风险：在不知情的情况下，已配置好的Agent配置被覆盖，造成工作和心智上的双重损失。
  • 对升级的信任危机：用户将问题直指“近期更新时出现的Bug”，表明一次不稳定的发布可能动摇用户对整个项目持续更新和可靠性的信心。

8. 待处理积压

以下为长期未响应但重要性较高的Issue/PR，提醒维护者关注：

  • （高优先级）Issue #2293： [Open] 重启后，多个agent下的USER.md被覆盖替换的BUG？ - 这是一个严重破坏核心功能（多Agent管理）的回归Bug。需尽快定位并合并修复PR。
  • （中优先级）PR #1338： [Open] 会话列表按时间分组展示 - 功能已实现，待评审合并。长期存在栈内会影响社区对项目响应速度的观感。
  • （低优先级）PR #1275 & #1276： [Open] CI依赖更新 - 两个dependabot提出的CI工具链升级PR，长期未合并可能导致CI流程的安全或兼容性问题，建议定期维护。

Moltis — moltis-org/moltis

Moltis 项目动态日报 (2026-07-11)

1. 今日速览

Moltis 项目在过去24小时内保持相对平静，未出现新 Issue 或版本发布活动。社区活跃度主要体现为 1 项 PR 处于待合并状态，该 PR 旨在增加对 GPT-5.6 系列模型的支持，是近期较为重要的功能扩展。项目整体处于“功能整合”阶段，无紧急 Bug 或技术债务需要处理，健康度表现平稳。

2. 版本发布

无新版本发布。

3. 项目进展

  • PR #1146 (待合并): Add GPT-5.6 model support — 此 PR 完成了对 OpenAI 最新 GPT-5.6 系列模型（Sol, Terra, Luna）的注册，并在 OpenAI 及 OpenAI Codex 回退目录中添加了对应条目。同时，该 PR 还根据官方文档更新了上下文窗口限制（API 端 1.05M 上下文、ChatGPT/Codex 后端 372K 限制），并替换了配置示例和提供者选择文档中的已废弃模型引用。这是 Moltis 与 OpenAI 最新模型保持兼容的关键步骤。
    • 链接: moltis-org/moltis PR #1146

4. 社区热点

当前社区唯一的活跃点是 PR #1146（无其他 Issues/PRs 讨论）。该 PR 的创建者（PeterDaveHello）已在两天前提交，并于昨日（2026-07-10）再次更新。尽管暂无评论，但其内容直接涉及 最大模型（GPT-5.6）的集成，表明社区对 Moltis 对最新前沿模型的兼容性有明确需求。

  • 关键诉求: 用户希望 Moltis 能够无缝使用 GPT-5.6 系列的强大能力（特别是超大上下文窗口），同时保持向后兼容性，避免因模型版本迭代导致现有配置或脚本失效。

5. Bug 与稳定性

过去24小时 未报告任何 Bug、崩溃或回归问题。项目稳定性良好。

6. 功能请求与路线图信号

  • 路线图信号: 尽管未正式发布，但该 PR 的合并意向与新模型支持功能暗示了 Moltis 的下一步发展重点：
    • 优先适配主流 LLM 提供商的最新模型。
    • 关注大上下文窗口场景下的性能稳定性。

CoPaw — agentscope-ai/CoPaw

好的，作为CoPaw项目的AI智能体与个人AI助手领域开源项目分析师，以下是为您生成的2026-07-11项目动态日报。

CoPaw 项目动态日报 | 2026-07-11

项目名称: CoPaw (QwenPaw) 数据时间: 过去24小时 (2026-07-10 ~ 2026-07-11)

1. 今日速览

今日项目迎来重大里程碑，v2.0.0 正式版本发布，标志着从 AgentScope 1.x 到 2.0 的架构升级全面完成。社区活跃度极高，过去24小时内 Issue 和 PR 处理量均超过40条，显示出社区对 v2.0.0 版本的高度关注和积极反馈。伴随新版本发布，也集中涌现了一批与 v2.0.0 直接相关的 Bug 报告和功能请求（尤其围绕 MCP、沙箱、记忆模块），这表明项目正处于快速迭代和问题收敛的关键阶段。总体来看，项目健康状况良好，但稳定性和用户体验方面的挑战依然严峻。

2. 版本发布

项目今日发布了 3 个版本，其中最核心的是 v2.0.0 正式版。

  • [v2.0.0] - 正式版发布

    • 链接: https://github.com/agentscope-ai/QwenPaw/releases/tag/v2.0.0
    • 核心变更:
      • Runtime 2.0: 基于 AgentScope 2.0 重构了内核，采用全新的架构、API 和运行时模型。这是本次升级的核心。
      • 破坏性变更: 这是从 AgentScope 1.x 到 2.0 的重磅升级，预计将带来 API 不兼容、配置变更等重大变化。
      • 注意事项: 已有多个 Issue 和用户反馈（如 #5948）询问升级指南、历史数据兼容性等，建议用户仔细阅读发布说明中的迁移指南。

  • [v2.0.0-beta.7] & [v2.0.0-beta.6] - 预发布版

    • 链接: v2.0.0-beta.7， v2.0.0-beta.6
    • 核心变更: 修复了主页文本、记忆回传 session_id 问题、通道单元测试等，是正式版发布前的最后打磨。

3. 项目进展

项目在 v2.0.0 正式版发布前后，完成了大量关键功能的修复和优化，主要进展如下：

  • 核心架构与 Bug 修复:

    • [Closed] #5938 fix(memory): propagate session_id into ReMe summarize tasks: 修复了命令触发的记忆归档丢失 session_id 的 Bug，确保记忆能正确关联到源会话。
    • [Closed] #5936 Revert "fix: per-message current time injection...": 因UI显示问题，回滚了之前关于“每条消息注入当前时间”的PR，表明项目对用户体验细节非常敏感。
    • [Closed] #5927 fix: add errors='replace' to _run_command for GBK compatibility: 修复了中文Windows系统下因nvidia-smi命令输出非文本字符导致的崩溃，提升了跨平台兼容性。
    • [Closed] #5942 bump the version to v2.0.0: 版本号升级。
    • [Closed] #5940 feat(website): Update homepage copy and visuals for QwenPaw 2.0: 官网首页升级，突出新版本核心功能。

  • 新功能与优化:

    • [Open] #5953 fix: use standard truncation hint for scroll-capped tool results: 提交了针对长工具输出截断提示的修复PR，旨在解决Agent因错误提示而发起无效 recall_history 调用的问题。
    • [Open] #5949 fix(mcp): apply access policy updates immediately: 修复了MCP工具访问权限策略更新后未能立即生效的问题。
    • [Open] #5931 feat(sandbox): add restricted token based windows sandbox: 为Windows系统引入了基于“受限令牌”的新沙箱方案，旨在解决现有沙箱导致的内存爆炸等问题。

总结: 项目已成功完成从1.x到2.0的重大架构升级，并围绕新版本迅速修复了大量Bug。当前进展聚焦于修复v2.0.0正式版的遗留问题，特别是在Windows兼容性、MCP工具权限和沙箱稳定性方面。

4. 社区热点

今日最受关注的 Issue 和 PR 主要集中在 v2.0.0 版本暴露的核心问题上，反映出社区对稳定性的强烈诉求。

  1. #5951 - [OPEN] Desktop shell sandbox: icacls timeout ... can't disable, must rollback

    • 链接: https://github.com/agentscope-ai/QwenPaw/issues/5951
    • 热度: 5条评论，创建当日即获得高度关注。
    • 核心诉求: 用户强烈反馈新版桌面壳沙箱导致pwsh窗口递归爆炸、内存占用达20GB且无法关闭，已严重影响正常使用，必须回滚。用户的挫败感和紧急程度极高。

  2. #5955 - [OPEN] [bug]: v2.0.0版本 webUI 智能体技能界面显示问题

    • 链接: https://github.com/agentscope-ai/QwenPaw/issues/5955
    • 热度: 2条评论，创建当日。
    • 核心诉求: 用户反馈 WebUI 技能管理界面存在严重的可用性问题，包括已激活技能显示不全（最多20个）、已禁用技能无法在列表找到等。反映出新版本UI设计在维护大量技能场景下的体验不足。

6. 功能请求与路线图信号

  • 热议功能请求:

    • 会话（Session）管理增强: [New] #5903 请求增加会话分组和导入导出功能。已有设计提案 [New] #5943 产生，这是社区呼声较高的需求，可能被纳入下一个迭代版本。

    • API自动化集成: PR [Open] #5930 为SSE响应增加结构化运行结果，方便外部服务（如Java应用）判断对话是否异常结束。这表明项目对企业级集成和API场景的考量正在加深。

7. 用户反馈摘要

从今日的讨论中，可以清晰看到用户对 v2.0.0 版本的复杂情绪：认可升级带来的新能力，但被随之而来的稳定性问题所困扰。

  • 痛点与不满:

    • 升级后稳定性下降: 用户 @wjt0321 (#5951) 在描述桌面壳沙箱问题时，直言“必须回滚”，体现了严重的挫败感。

    • 历史问题依然存在: 用户 @howyoungchen (#5455) 和 @renzhong424 (#5903) 提出的关于“时间注入”和“会话管理”的优化建议，在2.0版本中仍未得到根本解决。

  • 满意与期待:

    • 积极的设计参与: 用户 @nolanchic (#5943) 主动为会话管理功能提出了详细的设计方案，显示出社区对项目发展的深度参与和热情。

8. 待处理积压

  • [Open] #5952 - auto-memory fails with "No module named...": 一个在Windows桌面版上无法使用核心记忆功能的Bug，影响范围广，建议尽快解决。
    • 链接: https://github.com/agentscope-ai/QwenPaw/issues/5952
  • [Open] #5856 - Tool_call structure lost during context compaction: 一个在上下文压缩时破坏数据结构的重大Bug，可能导致Agent行为异常。
    • 链接: https://github.com/agentscope-ai/QwenPaw/issues/5856
  • **[Open] #5488 (假设编号，基于长期存在) - 几个长期未解决的“通道连接稳定性”和“飞书权限”问题 (#3502, #3432)，虽然被关闭为[closed]，但实质上并未看到根本性解决方案，建议重新评估。
    • 链接 (#3502): https://github.com/agentscope-ai/QwenPaw/issues/3502
    • 链接 (#3432): https://github.com/agentscope-ai/QwenPaw/issues/3432
ZeptoClaw — qhkm/zeptoclaw

过去24小时无活动。

ZeroClaw — zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw

好的，这是根据您提供的 GitHub 数据，为 ZeroClaw 项目生成的 2026-07-11 项目动态日报。

ZeroClaw 项目动态日报 - 2026-07-11

1. 今日速览

项目今日活跃度极高。虽然无新版本发布，但 Issues 和 PR 的更新数量庞大，其中包含了多项关键性 Bug 修复、社区驱动的功能请求以及持续的基础设施改进。尤为值得关注的是，数个严重（S1/S2）级别的 Bug（如 skill-review 崩溃、Gemini 功能调用失败、流式叙述重复）在今日被集中报告，表明项目在 v0.8.2 版本后正面临一波稳定性挑战。另一方面，社区讨论活跃，多项增强性 RFC 和功能请求（如 ACP 协议整合、多架构 Docker 镜像支持）获得了积极响应，显示出项目生态的健康发展。

3. 项目进展

今日共有 5 个 PR 被合并/关闭，主要集中于代码清理与局部功能优化，项目正稳步推进。

  • [CLOSED] #8957 refactor(skills): route pre-existing install/audit errors through Fluent：完成了对技能安装和审计错误路径的本地化（i18n）重构，提升了用户体验和多语言支持能力。
  • [CLOSED] #8956 refactor(skills): localize the pre-existing skills install error paths through Fluent：与上述 PR 配合，对技能安装错误进行了更精细的 Fluent 本地化处理。
  • [CLOSED] #8397 Feature: Expose per-cron-job uses_memory flag in CLI and cron_add/cron_update tools：一个重要的功能增强，允许用户通过命令行和工具接口手动控制定时任务是否使用记忆上下文，提高了系统的灵活性和可操作性。
  • [CLOSED] #8677 Feature: Add uses_memory check box to web gateway：与 #8397 关联，在 Web 可视化网关上增加了记忆上下文开关，完成了该功能从前端到后端的全链路支持。
  • [CLOSED] #8954 feat(container): add multi-arch Alpine/musl image via cargo-zigbuild：新增了基于 Alpine Linux 的多架构 Docker 镜像支持，特别是 musl 编译的静态链接版本，这对于在非标准 Linux 环境（如 Proxmox LXC 容器）或 Apple Silicon 上部署的用户是一大利好。

4. 社区热点

本日最受关注的议题集中在架构革新与外部工具集成方面。

  1. RFC: 整合 WebSocket 协议 (#8798)

    • 诉求分析：贡献者 NiuBlibing 提出将现有的 /ws/chat 和 /acp 两条并行的 WebSocket 通道合并为单一协议。背后的诉求是降低网关的维护成本和复杂性，并为未来支持更多客户端（如通过 ACP 集成的第三方应用）提供统一且强大的基础。该议题引发了至少 2 条评论，说明社区对此架构级别的变更非常关注。

  2. 外部 ACP 客户端连接验证 (#8958)

    • 诉求分析：metalmon 在使用 Mozilla Thunderbird 项目下的开源 ACP 客户端 “Thunderbolt” 连接 ZeroClaw 时，发现无法指定目标 Agent。这揭示了网关 ACP 端点在多 Agent 场景下的一个关键缺失：缺少 ?agent= 查询参数。该反馈直接来自真实的外部客户端集成尝试，证明了 ACP 协议价值的提升，也指出了其在生产环境中落地的具体障碍。

5. Bug 与稳定性

今日报告了多个严重 Bug，系统稳定性面临考验。

  • [S1 - 工作流阻塞] Gemini 功能调用失败 (#8934)

    • 描述：使用 Gemini 模型进行功能调用时，thought_signature 的缺失导致后续 API 请求失败，整个工作流程被阻塞。
    • 状态：新报告，暂无修复 PR。

  • [S2 - 行为降级] 流式叙述重复 (#8929, #8952)

    • 描述：在流式工具调用轮次中，已实时转发的叙述文本会被重复输出，导致用户看到两次相同的内容。问题根源可能与尾部空格处理和 unforwarded_narration 逻辑有关。
    • 状态：两个 Issue 高度关联，均于今日或昨日报告，暂无修复 PR。

  • [S2 - 行为降级] 循环检测器性能问题 (#8936)

    • 描述：循环检测器中的 hash_value 函数在每次工具调用时都会深度克隆整个工具参数 JSON 树，这在参数复杂时会导致明显的性能开销和内存增长。
    • 状态：新报告，暂无修复 PR。

  • [较高影响] skill-review Fork 崩溃 (#8654)

    • 描述：skill-review 的 fork 进程在工具密集型轮次后因切片越界导致崩溃（SIGSEGV），并可能拖垮主 Agent 进程。
    • 状态：已有对应的修复 PR #8680 提交，正在等待合并。这是一个关键的稳定性修复。

  • [中等影响] Telegram 命令菜单注册失败 (#8950)

    • 描述：当系统技能和工具总数超过 100 个时，Telegram 通道在启动时无法注册 setMyCommands，命令菜单功能完全失效。
    • 状态：新报告，暂无修复 PR。

6. 功能请求与路线图信号

以下是社区反馈中可能影响未来版本的功能信号：

  • 可观测性增强：FTDGRT 提出的为 OTel 导出添加 gen_ai.conversation.id (Issue #8933)，这是对项目可观测性能力的深度要求，有助于对复杂会话进行追踪和分析。同时，PR #8905 正在为仪表盘增加实时飞行中的提示计数器。这些信号表明社区对监控和调试能力有强烈需求。
  • 作为媒体提供商整合 ComfyUI (Issue #6563)：用户 metalmon 提出的将 ComfyUI 作为媒体生成提供商的诉求长期存在。这将为 ZeroClaw 提供强大的图像/视频生成能力，结合 gen_video 工具的远景规划，这是一个具有战略意义的需求，很有可能被纳入后续版本路线图。
  • 平台兼容性：PR #8954 添加的 Alpine/musl 镜像支持，直接响应了社区在非标准 Linux 环境下部署的呼声，是提升项目可移植性和部署体验的重要一步。
  • 外部客户端集成：Issue #8958 中要求的 ACP 端点 ?agent= 参数，是实现多 Agent 架构对外服务的关键。一旦实现，将极大增强 ZeroClaw 作为后端服务被第三方应用调用的能力。

7. 用户反馈摘要

  • 对文档的批评：用户 cr3a7ure 在 Issue #8810 中直言不讳地批评了 Telegram 示例文档的错误，用语尖锐。这表明文档的准确性和及时性已成为影响项目口碑的痛点，尤其是在新用户上手的环节。
  • 对上游集成的积极反馈：用户 metalmon 在 Issue #8958 中很高兴地报告了 “Thunderbolt” 客户端的成功连接测试，这对项目 ACP 协议的实现是一个极好的验证。这种积极的外部集成反馈是对项目架构方向的有力肯定。

8. 待处理积压

以下是一些长期存在或需要维护者重点关注的事项：

  • 高风险的“需作者回应”PR：多个涉及核心功能的 PR，如 fix(delegate) (#8571)、fix(tools) (#7960) 和 feat(gateway) OpenAI 兼容端点 (#8486)，均处于 needs-author-action 状态。这些 PR 对于修复漏洞和扩展核心功能至关重要，需要作者或维护者积极介入，推动其合并。
  • 尚未解决的严重 Bug：Gemini 功能调用失败 (#8934) 和 流式叙述重复 (#8929, #8952) 都是刚报告但影响严重的 Bug，目前尚无修复 PR。维护团队需尽快指派处理，以避免问题扩散。
  • 架构级别的 RFC：关于合并 WebSocket 协议的 RFC (#8798) 影响面广，需要项目核心维护者进行深入讨论和决策，以避免设计方向的偏差。

本日报由 agents-radar 自动生成。

item.content:encoded:
OpenClaw Ecosystem Digest 2026-07-11

Issues: 433 | PRs: 500 | Projects covered: 13 | Generated: 2026-07-11 02:47 UTC

  • OpenClaw
  • NanoBot
  • Hermes Agent
  • PicoClaw
  • NanoClaw
  • NullClaw
  • IronClaw
  • LobsterAI
  • TinyClaw
  • Moltis
  • CoPaw
  • ZeptoClaw
  • ZeroClaw

OpenClaw Deep Dive

Here is the OpenClaw project digest for 2026-07-11.

OpenClaw Project Digest

Date: 2026-07-11

1. Today's Overview

Activity remains high, with 433 Issues and 500 PRs updated in the last 24 hours. The project shows a healthy mix of bug fixes and feature development, though the number of open issues (244) slightly outweighs merged/closed PRs (179). No new releases were published today, indicating the team is likely in a stabilization phase between version tags. Key areas of focus include session-state integrity, memory management, and cross-platform delivery reliability.

2. Releases

None. No new releases were published today.

3. Project Progress

Merged/Closed PRs Today: 179 Significant progress was made across several fronts:

  • Platform Stability (Android): PR #103862 fixes Android's automatic resending of commands after ambiguous sends, requiring explicit user retry instead.
  • Telegram UX: PR #97828 fixes a bug where reasoning previews would remain visible indefinitely after a new message rotation (forceNewMessage()).
  • Agent Recovery: A fix for session worktree leasing (PR #103263) prevents in-flight workspace files from being garbage-collected during resumed runs.
  • Agent Diagnostics: A PR for silently swallowed context engine failures (#103904) ensures operators receive diagnostic signals when the context engine fails.
  • Infrastructure: A fix for Web Push broadcasts (#103607) prevents outbound request spikes during broadcasts.

4. Community Hot Topics

The most active discussions this week revolve around session state, security boundaries, and memory leaks:

  • Prompt Cache Issues: Issue #102175 (16 comments, P2) describes how embedded prompt caches break across various session boundaries, a regression impacting long-lived agents.
  • Memory Leak Crisis: Issue #91588 (15 comments, P0) details a severe memory leak in the gateway that grows from 350MB to 15.5GB before crashing.
  • Security & Secrets: The long-running feature request for "Masked Secrets" (#10659, 15 comments, P1) continues to be a top community demand, driven by concerns over prompt injection and credential leaks.
  • WhatsApp Stalls: Issue #84569 (10 comments, P1) reports that WhatsApp sessions stall on long model calls, leaving messages undelivered.
  • Multi-Turn Webhooks: Issue #11665 highlights that multi-turn conversations via webhooks are broken because a new session is always generated instead of reusing an existing sessionKey.

5. Bugs & Stability

Severity P0/Critical:

  • Gateway Memory Leak (OOM): Issue #91588 remains the top stability concern. RSS grows to 15.5GB, forcing OS-level OOM kills. No specific fix PR linked today.
  • Hosted Model Selector: Issue #101763 (P0) reports that the model selector on usemolty.com fails to persist, sending invalid model IDs to the API.

Severity P1/High:

  • Worktree Lease Loss: Fixed in PR #103263. Addresses a mid-run worktree deletion that destroys in-flight workspace files.
  • Codex App-Server Retries: Issue #83959 describes retry exhaustion before a replacement server is ready, leading to silent failures.
  • Broadcast Spike: Fixed in PR #103607 — prevents request spikes during Web Push broadcasts.
  • Codex Usage Limit: PR #103868 addresses a bug where Codex-backed turns hit usage limits but fail to trigger a model fallback.

6. Feature Requests & Roadmap Signals

High-demand features trending toward implementation include:

  • Execution Approval Deny-List: PR #101276 is a significant, nearly-ready PR adding a deny-list for exec approvals, complementing the existing allow-list. Likely for the next minor release.
  • Supervising Codex Sessions: PR #104045 (XL size) enables users to open, continue, and manage native Codex sessions from the gateway, bridging a major UX gap.
  • Durable Offline Chat (Android): PR #104089 (XL) adds a durable outbox for Android, preventing message loss/loss during process death and network reconnection.
  • Native Announce Targets: PR #101248 allows subagent completions to be routed back to the parent session instead of the channel, a core need for orchestrator patterns.
  • Portable Table Blocks: PR #103583 adds first-class support for structured tabular results across all channels and the CLI.

7. User Feedback Summary

  • Pain Points:
    • Memory Leaks: Users report severe OOM crashes on both macOS and desktop, noting that cron jobs and background tasks fail silently under memory pressure (#87109).
    • Silent Failures: A common theme is agents failing without diagnostic signals, such as context engine failures (#913904) and long WhatsApp model calls (#84569).
    • Billing & Cooldown: Persistent file-based provider cooldowns block users for hours even after they top up credit, causing frustration (#70903).
  • Satisfaction & Use Cases:
    • Sub-agent Patterns: Users are actively building multi-agent orchestration workflows and need better routing (announce targets) and visibility into queue status (#9797).
    • Rich Messaging: Users on Slack, Telegram, and Feishu are requesting richer message formats (Block Kit, configurable reasoning blocks, table rendering) to align with platform capabilities.

8. Backlog Watch

The following important issues remain stale or lack maintainer action:

  • Feature: Masked Secrets (#10659) — P1, 15 comments, 4 👍. No new fix PR. This is a high-impact security feature that has been open since February.
  • Filesystem Sandboxing (#7722) — P2, 11 comments, 4 👍. No new fix PR. Users need granular file access controls for safety.
  • Per-Agent Memory Wiki (#63829) — P1, 13 comments, 10 👍. Closed. This high-demand feature request for multi-agent isolation was unexpectedly closed today without a fix being merged.
  • Webhook Multi-Turn Support (#11665) — P2, 11 comments. No new fix PR. The documented feature does not work; a linked PR exists but is still open.
  • Node-Registered Agent Tools (#8287) — P2, 6 comments. No new fix PR. This enhancement for node extensibility has been gathering dust.

Cross-Ecosystem Comparison

Here is the cross-project comparison report based on the community digest summaries for July 11, 2026.

Ecosystem Overview

The open-source personal AI agent ecosystem is experiencing a phase of high-velocity maturation, characterized by a bifurcation between architectural stabilization (new releases and foundation refactors) and rapid feature iteration (model flexibility, multi-agent orchestration, and security hardening). The landscape is dominated by two major architectural families—the "Claw" lineage (OpenClaw, NanoClaw) and specialized frameworks (Hermes, IronClaw, CoPaw)—which are converging on shared requirements for context management, session-state integrity, and platform interoperability. Community feedback across projects consistently prioritizes reliability (memory leaks, silent failures, session misrouting) over new features, signaling a market that values production-grade stability for autonomous workflows. While no single project has achieved market dominance, the ecosystem as a whole is growing in sophistication, with deeply engaged power users driving requests for advanced features like per-conversation model routing and cross-session episodic memory.

Activity Comparison

Project Issues Updated (24h) PRs Updated (24h) Releases (24h) Health Score (Est.)
OpenClaw 433 500 0 High (Active dev, large community)
NanoBot 42 42 0 High (High velocity, bottleneck risk)
Hermes Agent 50 50 0 Medium (High regression fix activity)
PicoClaw 3 18 0 Medium (Focused fixes, low issue count)
NanoClaw 3 26 0 High (Major refactoring, many merges)
NullClaw 2 0 0 Low (Zero merged PRs, net-zero progress)
IronClaw 36 50 0 High (Intense stabilization, 15 merges)
LobsterAI Not specified 10 1 High (New release, rapid bug fixes)
Moltis 0 1 0 Low (Quiet day, single open PR)
CoPaw 43 45 3 High (Major v2.0 release, release-day surge)
TinyClaw 0 0 0 Inactive
ZeptoClaw 0 0 0 Inactive
ZeroClaw 19 50 0 High (Intense dev sprint, 5 merged)

Notes: Health Score is qualitative, based on PR merge rate, issue closure ratio, release cadence, and community engagement. Projects with zero activity (TinyClaw, ZeptoClaw) are effectively dormant.

OpenClaw's Position

Advantages vs. Peers:

  • Scale: With 433 issues and 500 PRs updated in 24h, OpenClaw operates at 10x the raw activity volume of any peer. This translates to a larger contributor base, faster bug triage, and broader ecosystem support.
  • Maturity: OpenClaw is in a stabilization phase between version tags, addressing systemic issues (memory leaks, session-state integrity) that define production readiness. Its backlog includes high-demand features (Masked Secrets, #10659) that signal deep community trust.
  • Platform Depth: OpenClaw has dedicated cross-platform delivery (Android durable outbox, PR [#104089]), rich messaging (Telegram, Slack, Feishu), and orchestration patterns (sub-agent routing, PR [#101248]).

Technical Approach Differences:

  • OpenClaw emphasizes explicit session management with worktree leasing (PR [#103263]) and Web Push broadcast control (PR [#103607]), contrasting with Hermes Agent's concurrent but less structured session isolation.
  • OpenClaw's context engine diagnostics (PR [#103904]) are more advanced than peers like NanoBot, which lacks analogous tooling (Issue [#4867]—cache miss adds 60s delay with no diagnostic).

Community Size Comparison:

  • OpenClaw's community is an order of magnitude larger than any single peer (500 PRs vs. ~50 for Hermes/IronClaw). This creates a virtuous cycle of faster bug discovery and resolution, but also a higher noise-to-signal ratio in issue tracking (244 open issues).

Shared Technical Focus Areas

Multiple projects are converging on the following requirements, indicating ecosystem-wide priorities:

  1. Session State & Data Integrity (OpenClaw, Hermes Agents, IronClaw, CoPaw, ZeroClaw):

    • Need: Session misrouting (Hermes #54527), worktree garbage collection (OpenClaw #103263), session isolation for A2A routes (NullClaw #974).
    • Value: Reliable multi-session workflows are essential for production agent deployments.

  2. Memory & Context Optimization (OpenClaw, NanoBot, Hermes Agent, IronClaw, CoPaw):

    • Need: Prompt cache breaks across session boundaries (OpenClaw #102175), KV-cache preservation for Ollama (NanoBot #4867), two-phase context management (Hermes #513), compaction recovery (IronClaw #5895), context loss on tool-heavy turns (ZeroClaw #8654).
    • Value: Reducing token waste and improving long-context reliability is the #1 performance bottleneck.

  3. Security & Authorization (OpenClaw, NanoBot, NullClaw, IronClaw, CoPaw):

    • Need: Masked secrets (OpenClaw #10659), unauthenticated /restart command (NanoBot #4776), A2A route authorization (NullClaw #974), MCP sub-tool allow/deny enforcement (CoPaw #5947), Slack DM misrouting (IronClaw #5943).
    • Value: As agents gain autonomous capabilities, credentials and data access controls become non-negotiable.

  4. Cross-Platform Reliability (OpenClaw, PicoClaw, Hermes Agent, LobsterAI):

    • Need: Android durable outbox (OpenClaw #104089), WhatsApp WebSocket reconnection (PicoClaw #3179), Telegram channel resilience (NullClaw #972), WeCom/DingTalk group task routing (LobsterAI #2306).
    • Value: Enterprise users demand reliable agent behavior across messaging platforms.

  5. Multi-Agent Orchestration (OpenClaw, NanoBot, PicoClaw, IronClaw, LobsterAI):

    • Need: Sub-agent announce targets (OpenClaw #101248), subagent model overrides (NanoBot #4623), agent collaboration bus (PicoClaw #2937), episodic memory for cross-session continuity (IronClaw #5974), delegated subagent collaboration (LobsterAI v2026.7.10 release).
    • Value: Hierarchical task decomposition and memory persistence across sub-agents unlock complex autonomous workflows.

Differentiation Analysis

Dimension OpenClaw NanoBot Hermes Agent IronClaw CoPaw ZeroClaw
Primary Focus Core reference platform, cross-platform delivery Lightweight local/cloud model flexibility Desktop UX, session routing Production resilience, architectural refactoring Multi-agent collaboration, enterprise IM Plugin ecosystem, web gateway, protocol convergence
Target User Enterprise power users, orchestration developers Individual developers, local model enthusiasts Power users with concurrent sessions Production-grade agent operators Chinese enterprise users, multi-agent workflows DevOps/infrastructure engineers, content creators
Architecture Monolithic with plugin channels Modular, lightweight core Session-centric Desktop app Reborn runtime (v1 retirement planned) AgentScope 2.0 foundation Plugin-based, ACP protocol
Key Strength Scale, maturity, orchestrator patterns Model flexibility (per-conversation overrides) Desktop session reliability Data-driven resilience (benchmark gap analysis) Enterprise IM integration, Chinese localization Plugin ecosystem, multi-arch deployment
Key Weakness Memory leak crisis (#91588), high backlog volume Performance with local models (Ollama 60s delay) High regression density post-feature releases Benchmark gap (30% vs. 65% on SWE-bench) v2.0 regression density, upgrade path unclear Multiple S1 blockers (Gemini, SIGSEGV), maintainer bandwidth stretched

Community Momentum & Maturity

Tier 1: High Momentum (Rapidly Iterating)

  • OpenClaw, NanoClaw, IronClaw, ZeroClaw: These projects show intense PR activity (50+ updated per day), significant architectural work (refactors, new releases, protocol consolidation), and high community engagement. They are moving from "early adopter" to "production readiness."

Tier 2: Release-Driven Momentum

  • CoPaw, LobsterAI: Both shipped major releases (v2.0.0, 2026.7.10) today, triggering a surge in bug reports and rapid patch cycles. They are in a "stabilize the new foundation" phase.

Tier 3: Stabilizing / Low Activity

  • Hermes Agent: High activity from regression fixing, but the project appears reactive—fixing bugs introduced by recent feature releases (Desktop Projects Paradigm). Velocity is high but primarily defensive.
  • PicoClaw, NanoBot: Moderate activity with focused PR trains (OAuth, performance). Both have clear feature roadmaps but lower community engagement than Tier 1.

Tier 4: Quiescent / Dormant

  • NullClaw, Moltis, TinyClaw, ZeptoClaw: Low to zero activity. NullClaw (2 open issues, 0 PRs) and Moltis (1 stale PR) may be at risk of stagnation. TinyClaw and ZeptoClaw show no activity and should be considered dormant until proven otherwise.

Trend Signals

  1. Model Flexibility is the #1 Feature Request: Across NanoBot (#4253, per-conversation models), PicoClaw (#3200, configurable fallback chain), and ZeroClaw (#8563, ComfyUI support), users demand the ability to dynamically route tasks to different models (cloud vs. local, cheap vs. expensive). The ecosystem is moving toward "model as a configurable resource," not a hard-coded dependency.

  2. Security is Becoming Non-Negotiable: Multiple projects are seeing user-driven security requests (masked secrets, per-caller A2A authorization, unauthenticated commands, MCP policy enforcement). As agents gain tool access and autonomous execution, security is shifting from "nice-to-have" to "blocker for production use."

  3. Multi-Agent Orchestration is the Killer App: OpenClaw (sub-agent announce targets), LobsterAI (delegated subagent collaboration), PicoClaw (agent collaboration bus), and IronClaw (episodic memory) all point to a convergent architecture: agents that can compose, delegate, and hand off tasks across sessions. This is the ecosystem's strongest signal for "what comes next."

  4. Context and Memory are the Bottleneck: Cache misses, tool-heavy context explosions, session-level memory breaks, and compaction failures appear across every active project. The ecosystem is still searching for a standard solution to long-context reliability—whoever solves this gains a significant competitive advantage.

  5. Desktop/Web UI is the New Frontier: Hermes Agent (Desktop Projects Paradigm, Kanban), CoPaw (v2.0 WebUI refresh), and ZeroClaw (web gateway, in-app upgrades) are investing in rich user interfaces. The CLI-only era is ending; users expect visual session management, approval workflows, and real-time progress indicators.

NanoBot Project Digest – 2026-07-11

1. Today's Overview

The NanoBot project remains exceptionally active, with 42 pull requests updated in the last 24 hours, indicating a high-volume development cycle. While the 17 merged/closed PRs suggest strong progress, a significant portion of the 25 open PRs remain in conflict or awaiting review, signaling a potential bottleneck. The community is focused on two major themes: improving model flexibility (per-conversation, subagent, and cron model overrides) and automating agent workflows (eager memory consolidation, goal queuing, and syntax highlighting). There were no new releases today.

2. Releases

None. No new versions of NanoBot were released in the last 24 hours.

3. Project Progress (Merged/Closed PRs)

The following significant features and fixes were merged or closed today, reflecting a focus on code quality, user interaction, and tool reliability:

  • Code Quality & Stability:
    • #4876 feat(webui): guide queued prompt with second Enter – Improves WebUI input handling.
    • #4877 feat(webui): highlight file previews and diffs – Adds syntax highlighting to diffs and previews.
    • #4832 fix(cli): handle CSI-u Shift+Enter instead of dumping raw escapes – Fixes a CLI regression related to terminal handling.
  • Feature Validation & Refactoring:
    • #4635 fix(tools): enforce exact edit_file line hints – Tightens the edit_file tool to use line hints as a strict consistency guard, addressing a major failure mode in editing benchmarks.
    • #4873 fix(dream): skip no-op periodic commit attempts – Prevents empty commits from the Dream feature, a direct fix for issue #4872.
  • Conflicted/Deferred:
    • #4623 feat(subagent): allow spawn model override – Closed (conflict) despite promising significant flexibility.
    • #4622 feat(cron): support job model presets – Closed (conflict) alongside the subagent model override, suggesting these two features share a common integration challenge.

4. Community Hot Topics

Most active discussions revolve around model flexibility and performance bottlenecks:

  • Model Override Requests (High Activity):

    • #4253 “support overriding model per conversation” (6 comments): A detailed user story from rombert requesting the ability to switch between fast cloud models and private local models based on task needs.
    • #4231 “feat: Add model parameter to spawn tool for subagent model override” (2 comments): Complements the above, requesting model selection for sub-agents.
    • Analysis: The community consensus is that users require fluid control over model allocation. The fact that the related PRs (#4623, #4622) were closed due to conflicts indicates this is a complex architectural change that maintainers are still deliberating.

  • Performance & Caching:

    • #4867 “Preserve exact prompt prefix to enable caching in Ollama and others” (3 comments): User The-Markitecht reports a severe performance regression where NanoBot adds 60 seconds to every turn with Ollama, making it "totally unusable." The solution involves preserving prompt prefixes for KV-cache hit rates.

5. Bugs & Stability

Several stability issues were reported or addressed today, ranked by severity:

  • High Severity:
    • #4776 (Security): /restart command has zero authorization – Any paired user can crash the entire bot process. No fix PR linked yet. Critical.
    • #4867 (Performance): Ollama cache miss adds 60s delay – Reported as making the agent unusable with local models. No linked fix PR. High.
  • Medium Severity:
    • #4835 (Bug - Closed): WebUI landing message sent to wrong chat – A race condition where a user's first message could be misrouted to a previous chat. This was marked closed with a fix.
    • #4634 (Closed - Fixed): edit_file target disambiguation – The dominant failure in the edit benchmark was resolved today via PR #4635.
  • Fix PRs in Queue:
    • #4842 (fix: MCP shutdown CancelledError) – A fix for crashes during MCP server shutdown (Priority: p1).
    • #4843 (fix: MCP reconnect crash) – A fix for gateway crashes during MCP session reconnection (Priority: p1).

6. Feature Requests & Roadmap Signals

Based on recent PRs and issues, the following features are likely candidates for the next release:

  • Dynamic Model Routing:
    • Per-Conversation Models: Issue #4253 suggests this is a top priority.
    • Subagent Model Override: Issue #4231 is closely related. While the PRs conflicted, the demand is clear.
    • Cron Job Presets: Issue #4378 (linked to the conflicted PR #4622).
  • Agent Workflow & UX:
    • Eager Memory Consolidation: PR #4626 (opt-in, append-only archiving) is a strong candidate for the next minor release as it improves long-term recall.
    • Guided Input & Syntax Highlighting: Features merged in PR #4876 and PR #4877 suggest a push toward a more polished WebUI.
  • Safe Automation:
    • Gated Sustained Goals: PR #4879 and PR #4844 indicate a strong move toward making autonomous agent behavior opt-in and user-authorized.

7. User Feedback Summary

User sentiment is mixed: high enthusiasm for new features is tempered by significant frustration with core reliability and usability.

  • Pain Points:
    • Performance: The 60-second delay with Ollama ( #4867 ) is a severe impediment for users running local models.
    • Security: The unauthenticated /restart command ( #4776 ) is a risk for multi-user environments.
    • Wasted Workflow: The "Dream" feature creating empty commits ( #4872 ) is a nuisance that was quickly addressed.
  • Satisfaction:

    • The prompt fix for the Dream feature (PR #4873) shows maintainers are responsive to concrete pain points.

8. Backlog Watch

The following items require maintainer attention due to age, criticality, or lack of resolution:

  • #4588 (Open - Performance): Context compression – A performance optimization PR that is 12 days old and marked with a "conflict" tag. Given the community's performance concerns ( #4867 ), this PR is essential for unblocking local model users.
  • #4205 (Open - Feature): Mailbox-backed subagent results – A fundamental refactor of the subagent system. This PR is 40 days old and has no recent maintainer comments, suggesting it may be stalled.
  • #4776 (Open - Security): /restart authorization – A security vulnerability with high impact. It has received only 1 comment and has no linked PR. This is a critical backlog item.

Hermes Agent Project Digest: 2026-07-11

1. Today's Overview

The project is experiencing a high-velocity maintenance cycle, with 50 issues and 50 PRs updated in the last 24 hours, indicating sustained engineering focus. No new releases were cut today, suggesting the team is consolidating "hotfix"-level patches before an upcoming stable milestone. No new releases were published today. The last known version appears to be v0.18.2 (referenced in issue #62394).

3. Project Progress

Seven PRs were merged or closed today, reflecting a push to stabilize session management and platform interoperability.

  • Session Routing (Critical Fix): Multiple PRs (#62134, #62144, #62413) were merged to address session misrouting (#54527) in the Desktop app, ensuring user input remains bound to the correct session during rapid context switches.
  • Auth & Config: PR #62423 fixed --ignore-user-config to properly bypass user-configured memory plugins (e.g., Honcho).
  • Context Management: PR #62422 introduced logic to preserve original message history instead of dropping it when context compression quotas are exhausted.
  • Desktop UX: PR #62409 was opened to fix inline image thumbnails by stripping markdown emphasis from MEDIA: paths.

4. Community Hot Topics

  • [Bug] Session Misdirection (#54527): The highest-traffic issue today, generating 3 concurrent fix attempts (#62134, #62144, #62413). Users report prompts typed in one Desktop session being lost or appearing in another.
    • Issue #54527
    • PR #62134
    • PR #62413
  • [Feature] Codex Compaction API (#33130): A user request to leverage the native OpenAI Codex Compaction API instead of Hermes’ internal compression logic. Closed as a feature request, indicating it was evaluated or slated.
    • Issue #33130
  • [Feature] Volatile Skills (#36656): A request for one-turn-only skill loading to reduce context overhead, receiving 2 upvotes.
    • Issue #36656

Underlying Need: The #54527 cluster highlights a core UX demand for reliable state isolation in the Desktop app, especially as users run concurrent sessions for different projects.

5. Bugs & Stability

The project is currently addressing a significant number of P1/P2 regressions.

  • Critical (P1):
    • Proxy Support (#5454): Closed today. Users behind corporate proxies could not route API calls. Fix was likely included in a prior release.
    • Telegram/Desktop Compaction Stuck (#48098): Desktop UI shows stale "Summarizing thread" status.
    • Bedrock Wizard Bug (#28156): Wizard accepts incomplete credentials (Bearer-only) which fail at runtime.
    • Gateway Message Routing (#54527): Fixed via merged PRs.
  • High (P2):
    • Stale TUI Content (#62170): Switching sessions in v0.18.1 shows outdated content.
    • TUI Stuck on Busy (#54756): UI remains spinning after task completion when the final response is empty.
    • MCP Process Leaks (#60385): Stdio processes for MCP servers accumulate on reconnect, wasting memory.
    • LSP Leaks (#25016): Idle Language Server subprocesses are never reaped, accumulating ~200MB per server.

6. Feature Requests & Roadmap Signals

Several feature requests signal the community's push toward context efficiency and multi-agent coordination.

  • Context Optimization: Two-Phase Context Management (#513) and Volatile Skills (#36656) both aim to reduce token waste, a clear UX priority for power users.
  • Concurrency: Per-tab CDP routing (#62338) and tab leasing (#62339) for the browser tool suggest the team is enabling concurrent agent operations.
  • Roadmap Prediction: Given the number of open PRs addressing session handling and state resumption, the next minor release (v0.18.3 or v0.19.0) will likely focus on stabilizing the Desktop/Sidebar "Projects Paradigm" launched in late June.

7. User Feedback Summary

  • Satisfaction: Users are engaging deeply with the new Kanban and Projects Paradigm features, but the high volume of regressions in session switching is causing frustration.
  • Pain Points:
    • Data Integrity: Multiple users reporting prompts being lost or misrouted (#54527). Fix is in review.
    • UI/UX Gaps: The desktop approval bar truncates diffs (#61249); email subjects are hardcoded (#46947).
    • Platform Issues: Users on Windows (TUI routing) and Linux (NVIDIA driver crashes #40077) report specific compatibility friction.
  • Positive Signals: The community is actively contributing high-quality test fixes (#62421) and refactoring for production safety (#62425, assert replacement).

8. Backlog Watch

The following important issues remain unaddressed or are pending maintainer action:

  • #25016: LSP Subprocess Leak (P2, Updated 2026-07-11): Idle LSP servers are never reaped. On a 24/7 gateway, this leads to a slow memory leak. No PR linked.
    • Issue #25016
  • #27284: Prompt Injection Scanner Gaps (P3, Updated 2026-07-11): The security scanner for memory/MCP tools misses multi-word ignore ... instructions variants. This is a security hardening item with no PR activity.
    • Issue #27284
  • #513: Two-Phase Context Management (P3, New 2026-03-06): A highly requested feature from the community leader (teknium1) that remains open with no PR despite being 4 months old.
    • Issue #513

PicoClaw Project Digest — 2026-07-11

Today's Overview

PicoClaw shows moderate activity today with 3 issues updated and 18 pull requests in motion, indicating a healthy development cycle. Project Progress

Merged/Closed PRs today:

  • #3179 — fix(whatsapp): reconnect after websocket drops — CLOSED/MERGED. This fix (authored by Alix-007) reconnects the WhatsApp WebSocket bridge after read failures, configures read deadlines and ping/pong handlers, and dispatches inbound messages asynchronously. Directly addresses the same class of failures reported in a now-closed issue.

Notable newly opened PRs advancing code quality and security:

  • #3248 — fix: bump Go to 1.25.12 — Remediates crypto/tls and os stdlib vulnerabilities in CI; expected to merge quickly.
  • #3246 — fix: security/robustness hardening — Verifies MQTT TLS by default (was InsecureSkipVerify: true), adds HTTP client timeouts for OAuth, and caps search reads.
  • #3245, #3244, #3243 — Three refactor PRs by corporatepiyush reducing string allocations in skills/loader.go and seahorse summary assembly via strings.Replacer and strings.Builder. Performance tuning for heavy-load paths.
  • #3242 — feat(whatsapp): add native typing presence — Implements channels.TypingCapable for WhatsApp, sending composing/paused presence.
  • #3241 — fix(auth): make OAuth refresh provider-correct and concurrency-safe — New companion PR to issue #3239, switching OpenAI to JSON body, enforcing 30s timeouts, and making refreshes atomic.

Community Hot Topics

  1. #3240 — Add typing presence to WhatsApp native replies (0 comments, but linked to PR #3242 which implements it) — [Open Issue](sipeed/picoclaw Issue #3240).
    Users report the WhatsApp channel gives no feedback during multi-second processing delays. The underlying need is user experience parity with other chat platforms.

  2. #3239 — OAuth refresh requests use incompatible provider semantics and can race (0 comments) — [Open Issue](sipeed/picoclaw Issue #3239).
    Paired with PR #3241 already filed by the same author. The problem — OpenAI requires JSON body but code uses form-encoded payloads — is being fixed reactively. Indicates a need for provider-aware middleware in the auth layer.

  3. #3205 — fix: support 9router gateway responses and add Linux ARMv7 build target (open 9 days) — [PR](sipeed/picoclaw PR #3205).
    A Raspberry Pi user reports 9router (an OpenAI-compatible gateway) returns nonstandard responses. Need: broader hardware support and gateway response tolerance.

  4. #2937 — Agent Collaboration Bus (open 49 days, 0 comments) — [PR](sipeed/picoclaw PR #2937).
    This is the most ambitious open PR: durable inter-agent mailboxes, collaboration threads, permission-aware specs. Low engagement may indicate need for design review or API discussion.

Bugs & Stability

Medium Severity

    Impact: Multi-provider setups (OpenAI + Google) experience token refresh failures under concurrent checks. Fix available in PR #3241.
    Risk: Auth failures under moderate concurrency.

Low Severity / Fixed Today

  • #3178 — WhatsApp WebSocket timeout (CLOSED) — Marked [stale], resolved via PR #3179.
  • #3248 — Go stdlib CVEs (crypto/tls, os) — CI-only; PR filed to bump toolchain from 1.25.11 → 1.25.12.

Feature Requests & Roadmap Signals

User Feedback Summary

  • Pain points:

Merged/Closed PRs (15 today):

PR Title Significance
#5950 Expose production LocalDev capability-port assembly to tests Critical testing infrastructure seam — enables harness parity
#5960 Raise default loop iteration ceiling 32 → 256 Prevents premature fail-closure on tool-heavy turns
#5895 Fix compaction failures after tool results Direct fix for #5838 — treats compaction errors as recoverable
#5967 Skip invalid available-extension manifests at boot instead of crash-looping Fix for production #5966 boot crash
#5954 RunFailureReason funnel foundation (phase 1 of 4) Error classification infrastructure, additive with zero behavior change
#5844 Tell agent to compute with tools, not in its head System prompt improvement to reduce hallucinated calculations
#5817 Don't treat decimal numbers as requested capability IDs Fix for spurious tool suppression
#5747 Slack unpair issue closed Reborn Slack disconnect functionality resolved
#5838 Context compaction error closed Fixed by PR #5895
#4640 Google Calendar list_events ordering closed Long-standing calendar API fix
#5835 "Jump to latest" positioning closed UI polish
#5828 Remove stale v1 test references closed Cleanup
#5837 Routine run action buttons not clickable closed UX fix

Community Hot Topics

  • #5959 — Reborn loop resilience (PR with DB migration, affects agent, channel, sandbox, CI). This is the largest architectural response to the benchmark gap, adding deep retries for provider 5xx storms, iteration backstops, and model-visible tool-failure reasons. The PR description directly attributes the 30% vs 65% gap to "the runtime discarding work rather than model quality."

  • #5973 — Per-server MCP timeouts + background-job bridge (XL, contributor: new). Lifts the hardcoded 30s MCP transport cap to configurable 5–21600s range, with background job support. Signals demand for long-running MCP operations.

  • #5974 — Episodic memory: cross-session summaries + recall (XL, contributor: new). Introduces automatic cross-session continuity with two-channel recall (push and pull). A major new capability for persistent agent identity.

  • #5972 — Per-turn tool retrieval + find_tools discovery (XL, contributor: new). Reduces prompt tokens by using cosine similarity over tool descriptions to select only relevant tools per turn.

  • #5948 — Assistant incorrectly reports GitHub extension as activated when only installed (5 comments, P3). Highlights a gap in status reporting between the assistant and the actual extensions UI.

Underlying need: The community and contributors are driving toward three major themes: (1) making Reborn production-ready with resilience comparable to competitors, (2) adding sophisticated memory and tool-retrieval capabilities, and (3) fixing real-world integration pain points (Slack, Google Sheets, MCP timeouts).

Bugs & Stability

P1 (Critical):

  • #5943 — Slack DM action posts to current channel instead of user's DMs. The assistant fundamentally misroutes direct message delivery. No fix PR yet.

P2 (High):

  • #5946 — Assistant mutates Google Sheet before checking Slack trigger availability. Destructive side effects before capability verification.
  • #5945 — Run fails with "model provider was unavailable" after long multi-tool execution (34 tools observed). Generic error after lengthy workflows.
  • #5944 — Slack DM delivery silently fails but run reports success. False positive success reporting.
  • #5885 — Approval notification opens action without showing approval message. User cannot approve/deny pending actions.
  • #5879 — Stale error banner remains visible after successful follow-up. Persistent misleading UI state.
  • #5836 — Routine fails on every scheduled run with "No thread attached." 0% success rate for scheduled routines.
  • #5834 — Slack disconnect request incorrectly rejected by agent. User cannot disconnect via agent interaction.
  • #5707 — Routine creation response exposes internal implementation details. Security concern.

P3 (Medium):

  • #5948 — Extension activation status discrepancy
  • #5891 — "Last completed" displays running run timestamp
  • #5889 — "Load older messages" button non-functional
  • #5947 — Thread deletion requires page refresh

Infrastructure/Regression:

  • #5966 — Boot crash-loop (CLOSED, fixed by #5967)
  • #5958 — Loop executor store I/O has no wall-clock bound (not compaction-specific, raised from PR review)

Feature Requests & Roadmap Signals

  1. Episodic Memory (#5974) — Cross-session continuity for agents. Likely for next release given XL size and new contributor.

  2. Per-User MCP Registration Store (#5970) — Tenant-scoped MCP extension management. Critical for multi-tenant deployments.

  3. Per-Turn Tool Retrieval (#5972) — Dynamic tool set selection to reduce token usage. Predicts increasing focus on cost optimization.

  4. Retire v1 Runtime (#5935) — Planned deprecation of legacy src/ code. Signals commitment to Reborn as the only supported path.

  5. Unify Reborn Dropdown Styling (#5938) — UI consistency for native vs custom SelectMenu components.

  6. GLM-5.2 Model in opencode (#5969) — User-requested model availability. Simple configuration fix.

Prediction for next version: The combination of #5959 (loop resilience), #5970 (MCP registration), and the v1 retirement (#5935) suggests the next release will be a major Reborn milestone focused on production reliability and multi-tenant readiness.

User Feedback Summary

Pain Points (real users):

  • Investment note workflow (#5955): Multistep workflows with sub-agents fail — missions hit tool-call limits or stop progressing. User report: "IronClaw fails on multistep workflows when asked to create an investment note by breaking the task into sub-tasks."

  • HTTP tool + third-party APIs (#5968): Generic HTTP tool fails with non-descriptive errors when connecting to services (Attio) without MCP support. User report: "The tool returns a non-descriptive error and doesn't support authentication or egress."

  • Large HTTP responses (#5741): builtin.http.save fails with OutputTooLarge for legitimate web pages.

  • Google Calendar ordering (#4640, CLOSED): "What are my upcoming meetings?" returned oldest events instead of upcoming ones.

Satisfaction Signals:

  • Active contributor community: Three XL PRs from "new" contributors (#5973, #5974, #5972) indicate growing adoption and investment.
  • The benchmark gap analysis (#5959) shows the team is data-driven about improvement, comparing against competitors (Hermes 65%, OpenClaw 40%) to target fixes.

LobsterAI — netease-youdao/LobsterAI

Here is the LobsterAI project digest for July 11, 2026.

LobsterAI Project Digest: 2026-07-11

1. Today's Overview

LobsterAI had a very high-activity day, driven by a major release and rapid bug-fixing. A single new version (2026.7.10) was published, incorporating a significant new feature for multi-agent collaboration. The project saw a surge of 10 merged/closed pull requests, primarily focused on stabilizing the Cowork space and fixing regressions in scheduled tasks and IM integrations. While community engagement on issues was moderate, the volume of PR activity suggests the maintainers are in a sprint to address feedback and solidifying infrastructure ahead of broader features.

2. Releases

Version: LobsterAI 2026.7.10 | Date: 2026-07-10

What's Changed:

  • feat(agents): support delegated subagent collaboration – A major feature allowing agents to delegate work to subagents, enabling complex, hierarchical task execution.
  • feat(cowork): add minimizable permission prompts – UX improvement for the Cowork interface, allowing users to minimize permission requests without dismissing them.
  • (Partial note) Further changes are included in this release.

Migration Notes: None specified. Users should update to benefit from the new subagent logic and UI fixes.

3. Project Progress

Ten pull requests were merged or closed today, indicating a strong focus on fixing bugs and hardening existing features.

  • Fix: Memory Indexing for All Agents – PR #2311 migrates FTS-only memory indexes for every configured agent, preventing data loss during startup.
  • Fix: Cowork Queued Follow-up Coordinator – PR #2315 ensures queued follow-ups are processed correctly even when the app is minimized.
  • Fix: IM Group Task Routing – PR #2306 and PR #2314 fix critical bugs in scheduled message delivery to WeCom and DingTalk groups, preserving case sensitivity and fixing delivery status false positives.
  • Feature: Folder Context Attachments – PR #2310 adds the ability to attach local folders as context in the Cowork prompt, sending the path instead of uploading individual files.
  • Fix: AskUser Minimize State Loss – PR #2312 resolves state loss when the "Ask User" dialog is minimized.
  • Fix: Windows Title Bar Logo – PR #2316 prevents the app logo from being compressed when the sidebar is collapsed.
  • Fix: Build Compatibility – PR #2309 ensures the build process remains compatible with ES2020 targets.

4. Community Hot Topics

Hottest Issue:

  • #2293 - [OPEN] 重启后，多个agent下的USER.md被覆盖替换的BUG？ (3 comments, created 2026-07-07).
    A user reports a critical bug where modifying the "About You" settings for one agent overwrites the USER.md file for all other agents upon restart. This is a major workflow blocker for users managing multiple distinct agents. The user suspects it is a regression from a recent update.

Long-Standing Feature Requests (Stale):

  • #1337 - [OPEN] 【功能缺失】会话列表缺少按时间分组功能 – A request to group the conversation list by time intervals (Today, Yesterday, This Week). This has been open since April 2026 and has a corresponding open PR (#1338).
  • #1392 - [CLOSED] 定时任务开关点击无反应，无法关闭 – A previously reported bug about scheduled task toggle buttons being unresponsive was closed today, presumably fixed by related PRs.

5. Bugs & Stability

High Severity:

  • USER.md Overwrite Bug (Issue #2293): This is the most impactful bug reported. It prevents users from maintaining distinct profiles for multiple agents. No fix PR exists yet. The maintainers are likely already investigating given the severity.

Medium Severity:

  • IM Group Task Routing (Fixed in PR #2306 & #2314): Several bugs related to scheduled task delivery to WeCom and DingTalk were fixed, including ID case-sensitivity issues and false delivery success reports.
  • AskUser Minimize State Loss (Fixed in PR #2312): A stability issue where minimizing the "Ask User" prompt could lose its state/results.

Low Severity:

  • Windows Title Bar Logo Compression (Fixed in PR #2316): A visual artifact.

6. Feature Requests & Roadmap Signals

The most active feature signal comes from the new release itself: "delegated subagent collaboration." This suggests the roadmap is focused on enhancing multi-agent workflows, likely a direct response to complex use-case requests.

Community feature requests with corresponding open PRs:

  • Conversation List Time Grouping (Issue #1337 / PR #1338): Highly requested UX improvement for managing many conversations. Likely to be merged in the next minor release.
  • Workdays Schedule Option (PR #1335): A quality-of-life feature for automation, allowing tasks to run only on weekdays.
  • JSON Import for MCP Server Config (PR #1336): A user-driven feature to simplify the configuration of custom MCP servers by pasting JSON.

7. User Feedback Summary

  • Pain Points: The primary pain point is the USER.md overwrite bug which is causing active workflow disruption for power users running multiple agents.
  • Dissatisfaction: Users have expressed dissatisfaction with the lack of time-based grouping in the conversation list, making navigation with many sessions difficult. Another pain point was the unresponsive scheduled task toggles (Issue #1392), though this appears to have been resolved.
  • Use Cases: Users are clearly leveraging LobsterAI for complex automation (scheduled IM tasks) and multi-agent orchestration (different agents for different tasks), indicating a power-user base that values customization and advanced features.

8. Backlog Watch

The following items remain open and have not received maintainer attention for over three months, representing a growing risk of community frustration:

  • PR #1276 & PR #1275: Dependabot CI updates that have been open since April 2, 2026. While low-risk, these stalls can lead to CI dependency vulnerabilities.
  • PR #1331: 功能增强：会话列表错误状态红点徽标 – A small, non-controversial UX improvement (adding error indicators to the session list) that has been open since April.
  • PR #1333: fix(agent,cowork): i18n attachment label, Escape close, delete guard – A triage of three user-facing UX fixes that have been stalled for months.
  • PR #1336: feat(mcp): 自定义服务器配置支持 JSON 粘贴导入 – A community contribution for MCP configuration that has not been reviewed or merged.

CoPaw — agentscope-ai/CoPaw

CoPaw Project Digest — 2026-07-11

1. Today's Overview

CoPaw has seen extremely high activity today, coinciding with the v2.0.0 stable release — a major milestone marking the full migration from AgentScope 1.x to AgentScope 2.0. The project processed 43 issues (20 open, 23 closed) and 45 PRs (20 open, 25 merged/closed) in the last 24 hours, indicating a release-day surge in both bug reporting and rapid patch delivery. Three new releases (v2.0.0 stable + two betas) were published. The community response is strong but mixed: while the release is celebrated, multiple critical regressions (sandbox explosions, memory module failures, MCP policy bypass) have surfaced, requiring urgent hotfix rounds.

2. Releases

Three releases were published today, with v2.0.0 being the headline:

Release Type Key Changes
v2.0.0 Stable Runtime 2.0: Kernel refactor based on AgentScope 2.0 (#5078, #4846, #5018)
v2.0.0-beta.7 Pre-release Homepage copy/visuals update; fix: propagate session_id into ReMe summarize tasks (#5938)
v2.0.0-beta.6 Pre-release Channels module unit tests; fix: envelope passthrough of tool result error state

Breaking Change: v2.0.0 drops AgentScope 1.x support entirely (#4727). Users upgrading from v1.x must migrate backend configurations and are advised to review the upgrade guide requested by the community (#5948).

Migration Notes: The v2.0.0 release bundle includes updated docs (#5932), but users report historical messages, logs, and memories may not be fully compatible — formal migration tooling is still being requested (#5948).

3. Project Progress

25 PRs merged/closed today. Key advancements:

  • Runtime 2.0 Shipment: v2.0.0 stable release finalized the AgentScope 2.0 migration (#4727 closed).
  • Memory Fixes:
    • session_id propagation into ReMe summarize tasks fixed (#5938), resolving auto-memory attribution bugs.
    • Revert of per-message time injection that caused ugly display (#5936).
  • MCP Access Policy: Fix for immediate policy updates — previously, policy changes were fire-and-forget and could be ignored if transport reconnection failed (#5949).
  • Docs & Website: Homepage refreshed for v2.0 (#5940), docs updated (#5932), news format refined (#5937).
  • Regression Tests: 43 targeted unit tests added covering install, tool-call handling, LLM timeout, and rm-protection bypass (#5813).

4. Community Hot Topics

Issue/PR Comments Subject
#5401 [CLOSED] 15 Console crashes on sessions with large tool-use history
#4727 [CLOSED] 12 Breaking migration to AgentScope 2.0 (now complete)
#5273 [CLOSED] 11 v2.0 pre-release bug tracker (retired with stable release)
#5951 [OPEN] 5 Critical: Desktop shell sandbox icacls timeout → pwsh recursive explosion + 20GB memory
#5947 [OPEN] 4 MCP sub-tool allow/deny policies ignored

Analysis: The highest-activity issues cluster around v2.0 release regressions. Two themes emerge: (1) Sandbox stability — the desktop shell sandbox has a critical bug causing infinite process spawning and memory exhaustion (#5951). (2) MCP policy enforcement — sub-tool access controls are not being respected (#5947), addressed by PR #5949. The v2.0 migration tracker (#5273) was closed as the stable release shipped.

5. Bugs & Stability

Critical severity:

  1. #5951 — Desktop shell sandbox: icacls timeout silently swallowed → pwsh recursive explosion + 20GB memory. No fix PR yet. Author states: "can't disable, must rollback to v1.1.12.post3".
  2. #5952 — Auto-memory fails: No module named 'agentscope.tool._builtin._scripts' on Windows Tauri desktop app. No fix PR yet.
  3. #5856 — Tool_call structure lost during context compaction → 400 errors / message count mismatch. No fix PR yet.
  4. #5947 — MCP sub-tool access policies (allow/deny) not enforced. Fix in PR #5949.

Medium severity:

  • #5950 — Chinese memory files trigger embedding 400 errors — truncation by character count, not token count.
  • #5946 — Agent falsely believes context was evicted → calls recall_history unnecessarily.
  • #5910 — Auto Memory Search generates malformed function_call for OpenAI Responses API → Cloudflare 502.
  • #5955 — WebUI agent skills display limited to 20, disabled skills hidden from view.
  • #5918 — prd.json format error loops forever, no way to break out.

Regression notes: Multiple v2.0.0-specific regressions reported — users considering rollback to v1.1.12.post3 (#5951). The rapid patch cycle (beta.6 → beta.7 → stable in 24h) suggests some issues slipped through QA.

6. Feature Requests & Roadmap Signals

Issue Request Likelihood for v2.1

#5453 KaTeX/LaTeX rendering in desktop Medium
#5954 Permission modes — user wants 1-time approval + whitelist Low-Medium — complex UX change

Roadmap signals: The merging of PRs like #5813 (regression tests) and active sandbox fixes (#5931) suggest stability hardening is the post-v2.0 priority. The session grouping feature (#5903) has a design proposal already merged, making it the strongest candidate for v2.1.

7. User Feedback Summary

Positive signals:

  • Community celebration of v2.0 release (#5945: "V2.0.0正式版本,终于发布了!☀")
  • Active ecosystem of first-time contributors (5+ "first-time-contributor" PRs)

Pain points:

  • No upgrade path: #5948 — "希望提供升级指南" (upgrade guide requested); users unsure if history/logs/memories are compatible

Underlying theme: Users are excited about v2.0's capabilities but frustrated by reliability regressions. Multiple reports explicitly mention "must rollback to v1.1.12.post3" — a clear signal that v2.0.0 has stability issues that need prompt hotfixes.
